title: Add prediction gate overlay on Step button for BST, Heap, Hash Table
description:
THE single most impactful practice feature. Currently the Step button reveals the next step passively. Instead: before revealing, show a prediction prompt derived from the step description.

For BST: 'The current node is 25. You are inserting 42. Which direction? [Left] [Right]'
For Heap: 'After extracting min, the last element 9 is at root. Should it swap with left child (4) or right child (7)? [Left] [Right] [Neither]'
For Hash Table: 'Key "alice" is being inserted. hash("alice") % 8 = ? [Type number]'

The DSStep.description strings in bst-ds.ts already contain the answer ('42 > 25 -- go right'). A parser can auto-generate two-option prediction prompts.

Implementation: PredictionOverlay component wraps Step button. Extends DSStep type with optional predictionOptions field. Shows overlay → awaits input → compares → shows feedback → animates.

Research basis: Testing effect (Cohen's d = 0.54-0.80) shows active prediction doubles retention vs passive watching. Brilliant.org uses this exact pattern.

Source: Kang 2016 'Spaced Repetition Promotes Efficient Learning', Brilliant.org lesson design.
acceptanceCriteria: Step button shows prediction overlay before revealing next step for BST/Heap/Hash
BST: direction prediction (Left/Right) at each comparison step
Heap: swap direction prediction during bubble-up/down
Hash: bucket index prediction during insert
Correct answer shows green flash + brief explanation
Wrong answer shows red flash + WHY explanation using DS property
Prediction is optional — user can click 'Skip' to see step without predicting

title: Add manual tracing mode for BST insert — user clicks correct child position
description:
Transform passive watching into active doing. In manual trace mode, the BST shows the current node highlighted and the value to insert. Two buttons appear: 'Left' and 'Right'. Student must click correct direction at each step. Wrong clicks show red flash with explanation.

This is 'You Are The Algorithm' for BST specifically — the highest-impact single DS for manual tracing because every step is a binary choice.

Extend to BST search and delete after BST insert works.

Research: Bloom's Level 3 (Apply) requires the student to USE the concept on new data, not just watch. Manual tracing is the bicycle — you learn to ride by riding, not by watching.

Source: Brilliant.org interactive exercises, VisuAlgo quiz mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Manual Trace toggle button in controls bar
BST insert shows current node + value, student clicks Left/Right
Correct click advances to next node with green feedback
Wrong click shows red + explanation ('42 > 25, so we go RIGHT')
Score displayed: correct/total decisions
After completion, auto-replay shows optimal path
Works for BST insert, search, and delete

title: Add 5 scenario-based 'Which DS?' selection challenges
description:
Bloom's Level 5 (Evaluate) is completely absent (0/5). Students never judge or choose.

Add 5 scenario challenges accessible from a 'Challenge' tab in bottom panel:

S1: 'You need O(1) lookup by username for 100M users. Which DS?' → Hash Table
S2: 'A task scheduler must always process highest priority. Which DS?' → Heap/Priority Queue
S3: 'A database needs sorted range scans over 500GB. Which DS?' → B+ Tree
S4: 'Count word frequency in 10TB logs, memory < 100MB. Which DS?' → Count-Min Sketch
S5: 'Spell-checker needs prefix-based suggestions. Which DS?' → Trie

Each presents 4 options. Wrong answer explains WHY the choice was wrong AND what the right answer offers.

Source: FAANG interview system design questions, NeetCode patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Challenge tab in bottom panel with 5 scenario questions
Each question has 4 multiple-choice options
Correct answer shows green + brief explanation
Wrong answer shows red + explains WHY wrong + WHY correct answer is better
Score tracked across all 5 questions
Questions randomize option order on each attempt

title: Add 5 comparison challenges — same data on two DS side by side
description:
Bloom's Level 4 (Analyze) scores 1/5. Students can't compare DS structurally.

Add comparison challenges:
C1: BST vs AVL — insert [1,2,3,4,5]. Which stays balanced?
C2: Array vs Linked List — insert at position 0 repeatedly. Which is faster?
C3: Hash Table vs BST — find all elements in range [10,50]. Which is efficient?
C4: Min-Heap vs Sorted Array — find minimum after 1000 insertions.
C5: Bloom Filter vs Hash Set — store 1 billion URLs, memory < 2GB.

Each challenge shows the question, student predicts, then the module demonstrates with split-view animation.

Source: Princeton Algorithms textbook comparison exercises.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 comparison challenges accessible from Challenge tab
Student predicts which DS 'wins' before seeing answer
Split-view shows both DS operating on same data simultaneously
After prediction, animation plays showing the difference
Explanation connects to complexity analysis

title: Add 3 debugging challenges with broken DS implementations
description:
Bloom's Level 5 (Evaluate) — judging correctness of code.

Bug 1: BST Insert missing empty tree base case → crashes on empty tree
Bug 2: Heap Extract missing bubble-down → heap property violated after extract
Bug 3: Hash Table delete sets slot to null → breaks probe chains in open addressing

Show buggy pseudocode + the visualization showing wrong behavior. Student identifies which line/concept is wrong. After identification, show correct vs buggy side by side.

Source: Common student errors from Reddit r/learnprogramming, GeeksforGeeks.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 debugging challenges accessible from Challenge tab
Buggy pseudocode displayed alongside broken visualization
Student selects which line/concept is wrong
Correct identification shows explanation + fixed code
Wrong identification shows hint pointing toward the actual bug

title: Add complexity recall quiz — quick-fire flashcard-style testing
description:
Bloom's Level 1 (Remember) scores 1/5. Students see complexity values passively but are never tested on recall.

Add a quick-fire quiz mode: show DS name, student types or selects the complexity for a given operation. 10 questions, timed (optional), scored.

'BST Search average case?' → Student types 'O(log n)'
'Hash Table Insert worst case?' → 'O(n)'
'Heap Extract Min?' → 'O(log n)'

Accept equivalent forms: O(log n), O(logn), O(log(n)).

Source: Anki flashcard methodology, testing effect research.
acceptanceCriteria: Quick-fire quiz accessible from Challenge tab
10 randomly selected complexity questions
Student types answer or selects from options
Accepts equivalent notation forms
Score displayed at end with correct/incorrect breakdown
Questions cover all 6 DS categories

title: Add feedback system with wrong-answer explanations using visualization
description:
Practice without feedback is useless. Currently zero feedback exists because zero questions are asked.

Design the feedback system:

Correct answer: Green flash + brief 'Correct! Because [DS property]' (1.5s)
Wrong answer: Red flash + DON'T just say 'Wrong' — use the visualization to show WHY:
  'You predicted left, but 42 > 25, so BST rule says go right. Watch:' → plays correct animation

Hint system (3 levels):
  Level 1: Rephrase the question
  Level 2: State the relevant DS property
  Level 3: Show the first step, ask about the second

Source: Brilliant.org per-answer feedback, Duolingo hint system.
acceptanceCriteria: Correct answer shows green feedback with DS property explanation
Wrong answer shows red + visualization-backed explanation
3-level hint system available on each question
Feedback persists for 2 seconds then auto-dismisses
Wrong answer feedback references the specific DS rule violated

title: Add per-concept mastery tracking with 5-star progress system
description:
Zero progress tracking exists. Students have no sense of mastery or growth.

Track per-DS mastery based on practice performance:
  0 stars: Haven't explored this DS
  1 star: Performed 3+ operations (explored)
  2 stars: Answered 3+ Level 1 recall questions correctly
  3 stars: Completed 5+ Level 3 predictions correctly
  4 stars: Completed a manual trace with <2 errors
  5 stars: Completed a scenario + debugging challenge

Display stars in sidebar next to each DS name. Overall progress: 'X/34 DS explored, Y at 3+ stars.'
Persist in localStorage.

Source: Duolingo crown/star system, Khan Academy mastery progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Each DS shows 0-5 stars in sidebar
Stars earned through progressive practice activities
Overall progress shown at top of sidebar
Persisted in localStorage across sessions
Progress dashboard accessible showing per-DS breakdown

title: Add progressive difficulty ladder with 5 levels per data structure
description:
No difficulty progression exists. All interactions are flat — same level regardless of student skill.

Implement 5-level difficulty per DS:
L1 RECOGNIZE: Multiple choice ('Which complexity is BST search?' → 4 options)
L2 RECALL: Free text ('What is worst-case BST search?' → type O(n))
L3 PREDICT: Animation-gated ('Will 42 go left or right from 25?' → choose before step plays)
L4 TRACE: Full manual trace ('Click every node on BST search path for value 17')
L5 SCENARIO: 'Which DS for this system design requirement?' + 'Find the bug'

Auto-advance: 4/5 correct → unlock next level. 2/5 or fewer → review prompt.

Source: Duolingo difficulty ladder, Brilliant.org level progression.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 difficulty levels implemented per DS
Auto-advance based on accuracy (4/5 correct → next level)
Auto-review suggested on poor performance (2/5 or fewer)
Current level shown per DS in sidebar
Each level's question type matches Bloom's taxonomy level
